Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Monuses and Heaps 36 upvotes, 2 comments
  2. Project Nomad – Knowledge That Never Goes Offline 187 upvotes, 29 comments
  3. My first patch to the Linux kernel 178 upvotes, 36 comments
  4. Building an FPGA 3dfx Voodoo with Modern RTL Tools 87 upvotes, 15 comments
  5. The IBM scientist who rewrote the rules of information just won a Turing Award 36 upvotes, 4 comments
  6. Reports of code's death are greatly exaggerated 25 upvotes, 2 comments
  7. A review of dice that came with the white castle 85 upvotes, 18 comments
  8. 25 Years of Eggs 171 upvotes, 53 comments
  9. Professional video editing, right in the browser with WebGPU and WASM 335 upvotes, 119 comments
  10. A Fuzzer for the Toy Optimizer 27 upvotes, 5 comments
  11. HopTab – Open source macOS app switcher and tiler that replaces Cmd+Tab 94 upvotes, 29 comments
  12. Flash-MoE: Running a 397B Parameter Model on a Laptop 202 upvotes, 73 comments
  13. MAUI Is Coming to Linux 8 upvotes, 0 comments
  14. More common mistakes to avoid when creating system architecture diagrams 76 upvotes, 30 comments
  15. Why I love NixOS 6 upvotes, 0 comments
  16. The Future of Version Control 67 upvotes, 28 comments
  17. Tinybox – A powerful computer for deep learning 560 upvotes, 323 comments
  18. The three pillars of JavaScript bloat 416 upvotes, 242 comments
  19. Chest Fridge (2009) 165 upvotes, 89 comments
  20. Bored of eating your own dogfood? Try smelling your own farts 243 upvotes, 149 comments
  21. Node.js worker threads are problematic, but they work great for us 45 upvotes, 21 comments
  22. Brute-forcing my algorithmic ignorance with an LLM in 7 days 59 upvotes, 33 comments
  23. I hate: Programming Wayland applications 117 upvotes, 81 comments
  24. Cloudflare flags archive.today as "C&C/Botnet"; no longer resolves via 1.1.1.2 257 upvotes, 207 comments
  25. Why Lab Coats Turned White 39 upvotes, 24 comments
  26. iBook Clamshell 48 upvotes, 35 comments
  27. Windows native app development is a mess 139 upvotes, 132 comments
  28. Zero ZGC4: A Better Graphing Calculator for School and Beyond 3 upvotes, 0 comments
  29. You are not your job 39 upvotes, 43 comments
  30. A case against currying 53 upvotes, 64 comments
